question: how does 369 works
content:
The 3-6-9 Spare Shooting System is a method designed to simplify spare conversion for bowlers. Its core principle is to maintain a single target, Arrow 4, for all spare attempts. Instead of adjusting the target or introducing ball hook, bowlers make all necessary adjustments by changing their foot position on the approach. The system emphasizes throwing the ball straight to ensure predictability.

Common mistakes bowlers make include trying to hook the ball or aiming at different targets for various spares, which adds unnecessary complexity and inconsistency. The 3-6-9 system removes these variables by standardizing the target and ball motion.

Mini-drill: To practice, establish your 'Zero Position' by finding where a straight ball thrown at Arrow 4 consistently hits Pin 1. Once this is determined, practice moving your feet 3, 6, or 9 boards LEFT or RIGHT from this 'Zero Position' while still aiming for Arrow 4 and throwing the ball straight As a result when you move 3 boards left from your ZERO POSITION you will hit the 3 and or the 9 pin. When you move 6 boards to the left with your feet, you will hit the 6 pin. And when you move 9 boards to the left, you will hit pin number 10. This works for both right and left handeds, one or two hands. If you stay on the ZERO POSITION you will hit the 1 or the 5 pin, when you move 3 boards right, you will hit the 2 and or the 8 pin, when you move 6 boards right you will hit the 4 pin, and finally when moving 9 boards right you will hit the 7 pin.
